name: Drip Tank
explanation: A drippy outboard spring-reverb tank — the long, boingy surf-amp spring sound, not a tight studio plate. Three parallel spring lines, each a chain of dispersive allpass filters (high frequencies arrive before low ones, giving the signature chirpy 'drip' and flutter) feeding a short damped delay loop. A built-in transient detector fires an excitation burst into the tank, so hard hits audibly boing. Controls: Mix, Dwell (send/level into the tank), Boing (excitation/drip intensity), Tone (dark to bright) and Decay (tail length). Bounded feedback — it never runs away.
